2606.09711.title: Proxy Reward Internalization and Mechanistic Exploitation: A Learned Precursor to Reward Hacking and Its Generalization
2606.09711.paper_summary: Reward hacking is usually studied after it becomes visible, once a model earns high proxy reward while failing the intended task. We instead study what proxy RL teaches before that failure appears. We introduce Proxy Reward Internalization and Mechanistic Exploitation (PRIME), a learned capability to assess task correctness, predict proxy acceptance, and reason about exploitable proxy--gold gaps. In coding RL environments with exploitable pytest rewards, we measure PRIME through chain-of-thought monitoring, direct probes, and activation-level concept vectors. We find that PRIME emerges in a staged sequence before sustained reward hacking, and that its current direct-probe score forecasts later hack onset and severity even when the visible hack rate is still low. PRIME also adapts when the evaluator changes, retargeting to whichever proxy--gold gap remains rewarded and persisting when gold reward suppresses overt hacking, and ablating its activation directions reduces hacking. Across checkpoints, in-domain PRIME tracks out-of-domain misalignment. Together these results suggest that exploitable proxy RL amplifies a proxy-internalization capability upstream of visible hacking, making PRIME a candidate early-warning signal for broader alignment risk.
2606.09711.paper_summary_zh: 獎勵駭客行為通常是在其變得可見之後進行研究，即當一個模型在未能完成預期任務的情況下獲得高代理獎勵時。我們則研究在該失敗出現之前，代理強化學習所教會的內容。我們引入了代理獎勵內化與機械性利用（PRIME），這是一種學習能力，用於評估任務正確性、預測代理接受度以及推理可利用的代理-金獎差距。在具有可利用pytest獎勵的編碼強化學習環境中，我們通過思維鏈監控、直接探測和激活水平概念向量來衡量PRIME。我們發現PRIME在持續的獎勵駭客行為之前以分階段的序列出現，並且其當前的直接探測分數預測後來的駭客發作及其嚴重性，即使可見的駭客率仍然較低。當評估者變更時，PRIME也會適應，重新針對任何仍然獲得獎勵的代理-金獎差距，並在金獎獎勵抑制明顯駭客行為時持續存在，而消除其激活方向則減少駭客行為。在各個檢查點中，領域內的PRIME跟踪領域外的不對齊。這些結果共同表明，可利用的代理強化學習在可見駭客行為之前放大了代理內化能力，使PRIME成為更廣泛對齊風險的候選早期警告信號。
2606.09711.author: Mohammad Beigi et.al.
2606.09711.authors: Mohammad Beigi, Ming Jin, Lifu Huang
